business.headline: Why has the Income Tax Department asked taxpayers to ignore advance tax emails?Many taxpayers were surprised to receive emails about advance tax payments mentioning "significant transactions". But soon after complaints surfaced, the Income Tax Department asked people to ignore those messages. So what exactly went wrong?

Buying the market dip? Why index funds may be the smarter betMarkets will rise and fall, but discipline often decides long-term returns. For investors unsure about timing the next move, index funds offer a simple way to stay invested and ride the market. Here's why they could be worth a closer look.

Sensex jumps 400 points despite ongoing Iran war. What is driving market today?The BSE Sensex jumped more than 400 points in early trade, while the NSE Nifty also moved higher. The rise comes as investors returned to buying after last week's sharp fall, even though tensions in West Asia remain high and oil prices continue to stay above $100 a barrel.
Sensex, Nifty open flat as energy supply crisis still weighs on Dalal StreetThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 98.52 points to 74,465.40, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 28.75 points to 23,122.35 as of 9:27 am.

Live: Black marketing in LPG, government must ensure supply, says Akhilesh YadavLPG crisis live updates: Two LPG vessels, Nanda Devi and Shivalik, carrying about 92,700 metric tonnes of LPG have crossed the Strait of Hormuz and are on their way to India, the government said on Saturday. According to the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Shivalik is expected to arrive on March 16 and Nanda Devi on March 17. Meanwhile, concerns over cooking gas supply are affecting several sectors, with restaurants, hostels and some companies reporting disruptions in kitchens and food services. Top private schools in Delhi have warned that lunch services could be affected if LPG supplies tighten further, while malls in the national capital have asked restaurants to operate conservatively and manage with about 80% gas supply. The government, however, has urged citizens not to panic, saying oil companies are delivering around 50 lakh LPG cylinders every day and supplies remain intact. Authorities have also stepped up vigilance against rumours, hoarding and black marketing of cylinders. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government measures and the impact across cities and industries in India.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Early signals from the derivatives market suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,257 at around 08:11 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Friday's closing level of 23,151.10.

upGrad to acquire Unacademy in all-stock deal, Gaurav Munjal to remain CEOThe acquisition signals a major consolidation in India's online education space, bringing together two leading edtech players under a unified, AI-driven growth strategy.

BNW's Smita Nair sees strong demand for UAE luxury homesSpeaking at a session at the India Today Conclave, Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong even as parts of the mid-market segment show caution.

Luxury, waterfront homes driving UAE real estate demand: BNW's Smita NairSpeaking at a breakaway session at the India Today Conclave 2026, Smita Nair said demand for ultra-luxury and branded residences remains strong, particularly among international investors.

Small teams will soon disrupt big companies in AI era: Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, the founder of OpenExO and founding executive director of Singularity University said rapid advances in AI are changing how companies operate and compete.

The next $1 trillion company could have only 5 employees: Singularity's Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Salim Ismail, founding executive director of Singularity University and founder of OpenExO, said the rapid acceleration of technologies such as artificial intelligence is changing the economics of innovation and giving small teams unprecedented power to disrupt large corporations.

India a bright spot amid global uncertainty, says AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large population, strong domestic demand and long-term infrastructure needs make it one of the most promising markets globally, says Arvind Tiku.
Renewables, urbanisation and demand to power India's growth: AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large domestic market and young population create strong long-term growth potential, says Arvind Tiku.

If valuables vanish from your locker, can insurance cover the loss?This is the second story in Lock & Key, a series examining how India's bank locker system works and what customers should know before trusting their valuables to a steel vault.

Either I collapse or get up and go: Anil Agarwal reflects on loss of his sonVedanta chairman Anil Agarwal said the death of his son Agnivesh has strengthened his resolve to continue working for India, urging opportunities for youth and calling for world-class education at home.

ED arrests 2 in Rs 183-crore online ponzi scam; accused returned from DubaiThe case was registered in December 2024 in Thane, accusing Sudhir Kotadiya, Umang Kotadiya, Jaysukh Sakhariya and others of running a fraudulent digital investment scheme that allegedly collected large sums from investors in Gujarat and Maharashtra.

Highlights: Two vessels carrying 92,700 metric tons of LPG en route to India, govt assuresLPG Crisis Highlights: Amid reports of commercial LPG cylinder shortage, Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge slammed the Centre, calling the crisis a result of the BJP government's "disastrous policy" and questioned PM Narendra Modi's foreign policy moves. Meanwhile, the shortage is beginning to affect services on the ground as well—food for the public at the "Didi Ki Rasoi" run by Jeevika didis at Rohtas Sadar Hospital has been stopped due to lack of LPG cylinders, and patient meals could also be affected if supply is not restored. Meanwhile, the government has assured that two vessels named Nanda Devi and Shivalik are en route to India with 92,700 metric tons of LPG cylinders.

Which four shipping routes control global trade? Karan Adani breaks it downKaran Adani explained that a large part of global shipping passes through four critical choke points. These routes act as key gateways for international trade and energy transport.

How is the global energy landscape changing? Karan Adani explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Karan Adani said the world has witnessed repeated disruptions in supply chains over the past few years, forcing countries to rethink how they manage energy and trade.

After Air India, IndiGo imposes fuel surcharge amid Middle East crisisThe energy crisis triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East is all set to make your air travel costlier. India's biggest domestic airline, IndiGo, on Friday announced it will impose Rs 425 to Rs 2,300 fuel surcharge on domestic and international flights from Friday (March 14).

entertainment.headline: Leonardo DiCaprio goes Oscar-official with girlfriend Vittoria Ceretti Leonardo DiCaprio and Vittoria Ceretti attended the 2026 Academy Awards together. This marked their first public appearance as a couple at the Oscars since they began dating in 2023.

Chinmayi defends criticism against Kamal Haasan, Rajinikanth over Vairamuthu row Singer Chinmayi Sripaada addressed the criticism she received after she called out Kamal Haasan and Rajinikanth for congratulating Vairamuthu on his Jnanpith Award. Nearly 20 women called out poet-lyricist Vairamuthu over allegations of sexual misconduct.

Oscars 2026: Priyanka-Nick's scary golf cart moment ahead of red carpet appearance Priyanka Chopra and Nick Jonas quite literally had a wild ride after a viral video showed the couple experiencing a tense moment during a golf cart ride. Fortunately, nothing serious occurred, as the driver quickly regained control of the vehicle.

Ram Gopal Varma says apartment where Bhoot was shot has been vacant since 2002 Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma shared a surprising detail about the apartment where Bhoot was filmed. The flat in Mumbai's Lokhandwala Complex, he said, has reportedly remained vacant since 2002.

KPop Demon Hunters Golden wins but speech cut short; internet reacts Viewers and fans expressed frustration as the acceptance speech for the Oscar-winning K-pop song Golden was interrupted at the 98th Academy Awards. The track won in the Best Original Song category.

Pawan Kalyan on Jana Nayagan vs censor board: Makers shouldn't have gone to court Pawan Kalyan, Deputy Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh, commented on the ongoing controversy surrounding the censorship of Jana Nayagan. He also talked about his film OG receiving an A certificate.

Greatest behrupiya: Sunil Grover's viral Kader Khan act earns Gajraj Rao's praise Sunil Grover's impeccable impersonation of Kader Khan on The Great Indian Kapil Show has earned him heartfelt praise from veteran actor Gajraj Rao, who praised him as the greatest behrupiya of our times.

Actor Trisha meets Parthiban's daughter at wedding amid public spat with him. Pics Actor Trisha's appearance at Eka Lakhani's wedding in Mumbai included moments with Keerthana, daughter of filmmaker R Parthiban, despite ongoing tensions with her father.

Michael B Jordan honours past Black winners in acceptance speech Michael B Jordan won Best Actor (Male) for his performance in Sinners at the Oscars 2026. He is the sixth black actor to win in the category.

One Battle After Another tops with 6 awards, including Best Film, Sinners bags 4 Winner's list One Battle After Another emerged as the Best Picture at the Academy Awards 2026. Directed by Paul Thomas Anderson, the actioner stars Leonardo DiCaprio as the lead.

Jessie Buckley bags Best Actress for Hamnet, bursts into tears after Oscar win Jessie Buckley bagged the Best Actress award for Hamnet, making her the first Irish actor to win the honour. She was nominated alongside Emma Stone, Kate Hudson, Renate Reinsve and Rose Byrne.

Oscars host Conan O'Brien ribs Timothee Chalamet over ballet-opera row Conan O'Brien's opening segment at the 2026 Oscars featured a playful jab at Timothee Chalamet regarding his recent comments on ballet and opera. Chalamet's reaction to the monologue quickly gained attention online.

Sinners cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw makes Oscar history Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history at the Oscars by becoming the first woman and woman of colour to win Best Cinematography for Sinners. Her win is a landmark achievement in a male-dominated field.

Oscars 2026 In Memoriam: Dharmendra left out; Rob Reiner, Catherine O'Hara honoured The 2026 Oscars In Memoriam segment sparked disappointment among Indian cinema fans following the omission of legendary actor Dharmendra. Several Hollywood icons including Rob Reiner and Catherine O'Hara received tributes.

Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans reunite at Oscars ahead of Avengers: Doomsday Hollywood witnessed a delightful Marvel reunion as Robert Downey Jr and Chris Evans shared the stage at the 98th Academy Awards, blending humour with nostalgia.

A rare tie in Best Live Action Short Film surprises all At the 2026 Oscars, both The Singers and Two People Exchanging Saliva were jointly awarded Best Live Action Short Film, marking the first tie at the ceremony since 2013.

Priyanka-Nick just made the red carpet a romantic hotspot. See pics Priyanka Chopra and Nick Jonas swooned everyone with their appearance on the red carpet at the Academy Awards 2026. The two brought glamour and served major couple goals to the prestigious ceremony.

Oscars 2026: One Battle After Another to Sinners, check out complete winners list At the 98th Academy Awards, One Battle After Another won six Oscars, including Best Film and Best Director, while Sinners took home four trophies, toplined by Best Actor for Michael B Jordan. Conan O'Brien hosted the gala at Dolby Theatre.

Peaky Blinders The Immortal Man on OTT: When and where to watch the film Cillian Murphy's Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man is set for its OTT release. Here's when the crime-drama arrives on streaming. Check out the cast and story details.

general news.headline: Iran clears India-bound gas carriers via Strait of Hormuz. S Jaishankar explainsExternal Affairs Minister S Jaishankar said Iran allowing two Indian-flagged gas carriers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz demonstrated that diplomatic engagement between the two countries had "yielded results."

Live: 3 Bihar Congress MLAs AWOL during Rajya Sabha poll votingRajya Sabha Elections Live Updates: Voting begins for 11 seats in Bihar, Haryana, and Odisha. Ahead of the polls, intense political manoeuvring was witnessed in Bihar, where the Chief Minister, Nitish Kumar, filed his nomination for the Upper House of the Indian Parliament, along with his party colleague Ramnath Thakur. A day before the elections, the opposition Grand Alliance moved its legislators to a hotel to avoid any situation of cross-voting.  The resort politics was also witnessed in Bengaluru, where the Congress alleged that an attempt was made to bribe its MLAs from Odisha. The party claimed that individuals from Odisha arrived at a hotel with a blank cheque and tried to approach legislators who had been brought to Bengaluru amid concerns of possible poaching. The results will be declared in the evening. To catch all the live updates of the polls, follow indiatoday.in

Forgive all, go in peace: Emotional farewell for Harish Rana, surrounded by familyThe video surfaced days after the Supreme Court allowed Harish Rana to die with dignity – a historic first court-ordered case of passive euthanasia in India. The court acknowledged the medical opinion that Rana will never recover and that the tubes that feed him and keep him alive are only prolonging his pain.
US jets strike military facilities near Iran's Chabahar free trade zoneThe Chabahar Free Trade Zone is located in Iran's southeastern Sistan and Baluchestan Province near the border with Pakistan.

Ahura and Asura: Iran and India are brothers linked by Gods and sacred fireHandcrafted ByOur Editors When we look at Iran through the prism of religion and see a Shia Islamic country, we negate its thousands of years of rich pre-Islamic Persian culture. A dive into the world of Zoroastrianism and Vedas shows us how Indians and Iranians have been sharing languages, Gods, sciences and a sacred fire for thousands of years.

According to the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Shivalik is expected to arrive on March 16 and Nanda Devi on March 17. Meanwhile, concerns over cooking gas supply are affecting several sectors, with restaurants, hostels and some companies reporting disruptions in kitchens and food services. The government, however, has urged citizens not to panic, saying oil companies are delivering around 50 lakh LPG cylinders every day and supplies remain intact. Authorities have also stepped up vigilance against rumours, hoarding and black marketing of cylinders. Massive fire breaks out near Dubai airport after drone attack, flight ops suspended Live UpdatesA drone strike hit a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport, sparking a massive fire that briefly halted flight operations.

Did Leonardo DiCaprio go Oscar-official with Vittoria Ceretti? See picLeonardo DiCaprio and Vittoria Ceretti attended the 2026 Academy Awards together. This marked their first public appearance as a couple at the Oscars since they began dating in 2023.

Accenture CEO says employees need to learn to use AI tools if they want promotionsAccenture CEO Julie Sweet says employees who want promotions must learn to use AI tools as the company makes AI central to its workflow. She also said entry-level hiring continues as young graduates bring strong AI skills.

US aircraft carriers add an edge to Iran offensive. Where does India stand?The US is planning to deploy a third aircraft carrier in the Middle East amid the war with Iran. Aircraft carriers, one of the most expensive defence systems, are floating military bases that project power. Here's how aircraft carriers are used and where India stands.

Election Commission removes Bengal DGP, Chief Secretary; appoints new facesThe Election Commission reshuffled West Bengal Police leadership by transferring the DGP and three senior officers, appointing Siddh Nath Gupta as the new DGP in-charge.

Kia launches sub-Rs 10 lakh diesel and turbo-petrol automatics for SonetKia Sonet gets a MY26 update in India, introducing diesel 6AT and turbo-petrol 7DCT variants priced under Rs 10 lakh, expanding automatic accessibility in the competitive compact SUV segment.

US plans to announce multi-nation coalition to escort ships through Hormuz: WSJSeveral countries, including some very close allies of the US, have appeared evasive on US President Donald Trump's open call to nations to send warships to enable container vessels to pass through the war-hit Strait of Hormuz unharmed.

We dream when we sleep, so why do we forget them when we wake up?In today's The Daily Why, a new series from India Today Science, we try to understand the mechanism that triggers dreams, and the one that makes us forget them.

One Battle After Another tops with 6 awards, including Best Film, Sinners bags 4 Winner's listOne Battle After Another emerged as the Best Picture at the Academy Awards 2026. Directed by Paul Thomas Anderson, the actioner stars Leonardo DiCaprio as the lead.

Feathers, florals and some drama on the Oscars 2026 red carpetAmong the fashion standouts at the Oscars 2026 were Nicole Kidman, Teyana Taylor, Priyanka Chopra, Emma Stone, Audrey Nuna, Anne Hathaway and Pedro Pascal.

Trump warns of very bad future for Nato if allies fail to help reopen HormuzTrump urged allies to back Washington's push to reopen the vital oil route amid the Iran conflict, warning of a 'very bad future' for Nato if their response is negative.

Live: Israel launches limited ground ops against Hezbollah in LebanonUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: In yet another significant escalation in the Middle East, a drone struck near Dubai International Airport, causing a blaze that was eventually contained but left flight operations temporarily suspended. The blaze was caused by the projectile hitting a fuel tank in the vicinity of the major airport. In another development, US President Donald Trump has said that he is in talks with about seven countries to form a coalition to escort merchant ships through the Strait of Hormuz unharmed. He, however, declined to name the countries. But he said he won't forget the countries that decline to help. Coming to the financial toll of the growing conflict, the price of US crude oil has gone above USD 100 per barrel. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

From FOMO to financial stress: What is driving anxiety in Gen ZGen Z is growing up in a world shaped by social media, economic uncertainty and constant global crises. Experts say these pressures are creating a generation that feels more anxious than ever before.

Priyanka-Nick just made the red carpet a romantic hotspot. See picsPriyanka Chopra and Nick Jonas swooned everyone with their appearance on the red carpet at the Academy Awards 2026. The two brought glamour and served major couple goals to the prestigious ceremony.

10 killed in massive fire at state-run hospital in Cuttack; short circuit suspectedTen patients were killed and 11 hospital staff injured in a massive fire at SCB Medical College and Hospital in Odisha's Cuttack. A short circuit in the ICU's AC or medical equipment is suspected to have caused the blaze.

Ex-India coach Rahul Dravid proud of Sanju Samson's comeback journey in T20 World CupRahul Dravid has praised Sanju Samson's remarkable comeback during India's triumphant 2026 T20 World Cup campaign. The wicketkeeper-batter delivered decisive performances in the knockout stages and emerged as Player of the Tournament.

sports.headline: Who is Max Dowman? Arsenal's wonderkid now Premier League's youngest scorer Arsenal teenager Max Dowman made Premier League history with a stunning goal against Everton. At just 16 years and 73 days, the winger became the youngest goalscorer the league has ever seen.

Can Carrick bring the good times back to Manchester United? Rooney shares his stance Seven wins from nine games have taken Manchester United from sixth to third under Michael Carrick, with Wayne Rooney and fans growing louder in their calls for him to be handed the job permanently.

Tried to make Gambhir laugh many times during T20 World Cup, reveals Suryakumar Yadav Suryakumar Yadav revealed India's players tried repeatedly to make coach Gautam Gambhir laugh during the T20 World Cup 2026, finally succeeding after their title win in Ahmedabad on Sunday night.

Ahmedabad curse behind him, Suryakumar wishes he could replay 2023 World Cup final Suryakumar Yadav says lifting the T20 World Cup 2026 trophy in Ahmedabad has finally put to rest the pain of the 2023 ODI World Cup final defeat at the same venue, the memory that haunted him most.

Watch: KKR injury worries grow as Harshit Rana spotted on crutches, IPL 2026 in doubt KKR's preparations for IPL 2026 could suffer a major blow as fast bowler Harshit Rana's injury concerns grow. The young pacer was recently seen on crutches, raising doubts over his availability for the upcoming season.

Ex-India coach Rahul Dravid proud of Sanju Samson's comeback journey in T20 World Cup Rahul Dravid has praised Sanju Samson's remarkable comeback during India's triumphant 2026 T20 World Cup campaign. The wicketkeeper-batter delivered decisive performances in the knockout stages and emerged as Player of the Tournament.

India winning the 2027 ODI World Cup is the ultimate goal: Shubman Gill India captain Shubman Gill has set his sights on the 2027 ODI World Cup, calling it the ultimate target for the team after their heartbreak in the 2023 final. The skipper believes India have another shot at glory.

No Messi vs Yamal as UEFA cancels Spain vs Argentina Finalissima over Middle East war Football fans will have to wait longer for the dream clash between Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal. The 2026 Finalissima between Spain and Argentina has been cancelled due to escalating tensions in the Middle East.

Sinner outlasts Medvedev in gritty Indian Wells final, joins hard-court elites Jannik Sinner edged Daniil Medvedev in a tense Indian Wells final to complete a rare hard-court Masters sweep. The Italian joined Novak Djokovic and Roger Federer as the only men to win all six ATP Masters 1000 hard-court titles.

Pakistan slump further after T20 World Cup debacle, lose ODI series to Bangladesh BAN vs PAK, 3rd ODI: Bangladesh beat Pakistan by 11 runs in the third and final ODI on Sunday at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium to win the series 2-1.

Samson reveals Sooryavanshi's carefree chat with Dravid: Pehla hi uda denge During the Naman Awards, Sanju Samson revealed that during IPL 2025, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi confidently told Rahul Dravid he would simply play his natural game, even planning to go aerial on the first opportunity.

Gulveer Singh shatters national record with bronze in New York City half marathonIndia's Gulveer Singh made history at the NYC Half Marathon 2026, winning bronze and breaking the national men's half-marathon record with a time of 59:42, becoming the first Indian to run under one hour.

Shubman Gill, Smriti Mandhana win BCCI's Cricketer of the Year awardsNaman Awards 2026: On Sunday, March 15, Shubman Gill won the Polly Umrigar Award for Best International Men's Cricketer of the Year, while Smriti Mandhana was named Women's Cricketer of the Year.

BCCI felicitates India's 5 World Cup-winning teams in New DelhiThe BCCI honoured India's five World Cup-winning teams at the Naman Awards 2026 in New Delhi, with the U19 women's, U19 men's, Champions Trophy, Women's World Cup and Men's T20 World Cup-winning sides all being felicitated.

Done with losing big finals: Sabalenka eyes revenge against Rybakina at Indian WellsIndian Wells: World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka has said she will give it her best shot when she takes on Elena Rybakina in Sunday's final. Sabalenka has lost each of her last four finals against Rybakina.

Fuming Max Verstappen tears into new F1 era after Shanghai GP retirementMax Verstappen launched a fierce attack on Formula One's new regulations after retiring from the Chinese Grand Prix with a power unit failure, calling the rules "fundamentally flawed" and warning they could ruin the sport.

Amelia Kerr equals McCullum in elite list, continues dream run as New Zealand captainNZW vs SAW: Amelia Kerr continued her red-hot run as captain, smashing 78 off 44 balls against South Africa, equalling Brendon McCullum and leading the hosts to an 80-run win in the first T20I.

Gambhir's 2014 IPL nightmare helped Abhishek bounce back in T20 World Cup finalGautam Gambhir reveals he used his own four-duck IPL 2014 nightmare to back Abhishek Sharma through three consecutive ducks before his match-winning 52 in the T20 World Cup final.

South Africa twist knife on New Zealand's T20 World Cup heartbreak with 7-wicket routSouth Africa's debutants bowled New Zealand out for 91 at Bay Oval to win the first T20I by seven wickets, piling more misery on the World Cup finalists just days after their final defeat.

Is Vaibhav Sooryavanshi riding on luck? Ex-India star predicts tougher IPL 2026IPL 2026: Former India cricketer Robin Uthappa believes young batting sensation Vaibhav Suryavanshi will face a tougher challenge in IPL 2026. The 14-year-old prodigy impressed last season, but Uthappa expects bowlers to test him more this year.

Pakistan U19 coach Sarfaraz Ahmed announces retirement from international cricketSarfaraz Ahmed has retired from international cricket, bringing the curtain down on a decorated career for Pakistan in which he scored 6,164 runs and led the team to the 2017 ICC Champions Trophy title.

F1 Shanghai GP: Mercedes' Antonelli takes first win, Hamilton secures first podiumKimi Antonelli won the 2026 Chinese Grand Prix for Mercedes, with Lewis Hamilton taking his first Ferrari podium. McLaren's Norris and Piastri both failed to start in Shanghai.

KKR should not have let Shreyas Iyer go: Anil Kumble slams team's retention policyAnil Kumble believes Kolkata Knight Riders weakened their own title-winning core by releasing Shreyas Iyer and Phil Salt after their 2024 triumph. The former India captain criticised the franchise's inconsistent retention strategy.

New kit in 7 hours: Indian football body hits back at critics over Women's Asian Cup rowAIFF deputy secretary general M Satyanarayan says the federation replaced the Indian women's team's ill-fitting AFC Women's Asian Cup kits within seven hours, but the effort went unrecognised.

Robot, national treasure: Suryakumar doesn't hold back on his praise for BumrahT20 World Cup 2026: Suryakumar Yadav called Jasprit Bumrah a "robot" and "national treasure" after his match-winning 4/15 in the T20 World Cup final helped India defend their title against New Zealand.

The Hands of God that shaped Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup redemptionSanju Samson began the year battling doubt and uncertainty. Months later, he stood as India's T20 World Cup hero, lifted by belief, mentorship and a support system that helped him rediscover the fearless batter within.

3 Iran women's football team members head home after rejecting Australia asylumThree members of Iran's women's football team who had accepted humanitarian visas in Australia have now chosen to return home. Their departure leaves only three players from the initial group who decided to stay behind.

Rohit Sharma hopeful India will extend cricket dominance: T20 World Cup just a startRohit Sharma believes India's recent success in ICC tournaments could be the start of a strong phase for the national teams. The India captain praised both men's and women's teams and hopes the winning run continues.

Watch: Real Madrid's Arda Guler ties LaLiga record with stunning 74-yard goalArda Guler lit up the Santiago Bernabeu with a sensational long-range strike as Real Madrid beat Elche. The Turkish youngster's outrageous effort from distance matched the longest goal ever scored in LaLiga history.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
